page: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is a dedicated CPU virtual server built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical AMD EPYC Milan cores with multithreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 512 KB L1d cache, 8 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The system is provisioned with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 600 GB of local SSD storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier. Multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng falls in the average range, as do general processing benchmarks like Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Cryptographic performance is a notable strength, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets. Memory performance is also robust, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top 25%, while large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain average.

For specialized tasks, the ccx53 demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds during LLM inference with small 135M models, though performance drops to the bottom 10% when generating text with large 70B models due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. The server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, where its dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.0 GHz. Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM and 600 GB of local SSD storage, this instance delivers balanced compute and memory resources without GPU acceleration. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong cryptographic execution, alongside solid memory performance. While multi-core processing, database operations, and web serving throughput align with average tiers, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures stable execution. This configuration is highly suitable for software compilation, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring dedicated, non-shared CPU resources.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx53 is an x86_64 server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 600 GB SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for general-purpose and compute workloads.

tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC virtual server offering balanced compute, 128 GB RAM, and local SSD storage for consistent general-purpose workloads.

128.0 GB of RAM and 600 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
